refactor: replace unused mesh/swarm toggles with palette picker in mode strip

Telemetry shows mesh and swarm modes have zero usage. Replaced them
with a palette cycle button that surfaces the color palette feature
(previously keyboard-only via Y key) directly in the mode strip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add button-abort to blocking wait loops via emergency_stop()

Pressing the user button while a profile is active now causes the robot
to stop immediately rather than continuing to run indefinitely.

- motion.h: add emergency_stop() which calls reset_drive_system() then
  disable_drive(). This is the shared safe-state entry point for the
  motion layer.
- motion.h: spin_turn() forward-bleed loop and wait_until_position()
  both check switches.button_pressed() and call emergency_stop() on press.
- profile.h: wait_until_finished() checks switches.button_pressed() and
  calls this->stop() (target speed → 0, state → PS_FINISHED), causing
  the systick PID to ramp motors down. Returns immediately so the caller
  can handle the abort.
- mouse.h: panic() now calls motion.emergency_stop() instead of the two
  separate reset_drive_system()/disable_drive() calls. Sensor shutdown
  and operator interaction (LED blink, button wait) remain in panic().

Closes #25 (FR-10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bug: PRs still appear in Ready to Merge while Copilot review is pending (Forge-6sc) (#152)

* fix: prevent PRs from appearing in Ready to Merge while Copilot review is pending

The race condition where new PRs briefly appeared in Ready to Merge before
GitHub assigned Copilot as a reviewer has regressed. The root cause is that
InsertPR defaulted has_pending_reviews to 0 (no pending reviews), and the
immediate CheckStatusLight call after PR creation ran before GitHub had
assigned reviewers asynchronously.

Three-part fix:
1. InsertPR now explicitly sets has_pending_reviews=1, so new PRs assume
   reviews are pending until bellows confirms otherwise ("guilty until
   proven innocent").
2. CheckStatusLight in ghpr.Create() no longer clears has_pending_reviews
   to 0 — it preserves the safe default and lets bellows be the
   authoritative source for clearing the flag.
3. The daemon merge handler's live readiness check now also verifies
   HasPendingReviewRequests(), adding defense in depth.

Bead: Forge-6sc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-6sc

- Update comment to accurately describe that CheckStatusLight only checks
  merge conflict state, not unresolved threads; clarify Bellows is the
  authoritative source for threads and has_pending_reviews
- Inline the two fields actually used from CheckStatusLight result instead
  of calling MergeabilityFromStatus (which includes HasPendingReviews),
  making it explicit that pending reviews are intentionally kept at the
  safe default (true) regardless of the light check result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pass correct language to whisper — eliminates ~2x inference overhead

Root cause: commit b06cc72b changed params.language from "en" to "auto"
in jni.c. For English-only models (.en.), "auto" forces whisper.cpp to
run a language detection encoder pass before transcription, roughly
doubling inference time. On the user's device this turned a ~48s
transcription into ~96s, exceeding the 60s timeout every time.

Fix: detect English-only models from the filename (.en.) and pass "en"
directly, skipping the unnecessary language detection pass. Multilingual
models still use "auto" for language detection.

Also reduces timeout from 180s to 120s since inference should now be
~half what it was with auto-detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: receiver workflow architecture — adapter trigger + pipeline + call-orch (1.0.38)

Per Sandro Pereira: when a receive location uses a non-default pipeline (flat file
decode, archiving, custom actions), generate a RECEIVER WORKFLOW that owns the
adapter trigger, runs the pipeline processing logic, then calls the orchestration
via the native Call Workflow action.

Architecture:
  Rcv_{name}:   adapter trigger → pipeline steps → Call Orchestration (Workflow action)
  Orchestration: Request trigger (called by receiver) + business logic + Response
  Pipeline_{name}: standalone Request workflow (fallback when no BindingInfo.xml)

Changes:
- Add buildAdapterTrigger(rl) — builds IntegrationTrigger from ReceiveLocation
- Add buildPipelineSteps(pipeline) — shared helper for pipeline step generation
- Refactor buildPackage() into 3 passes:
    1a. Receiver workflows: when bindings exist + non-default pipeline in receive location
    1b. Orchestration workflows: Request trigger when receivers generated, else adapter
    1c. Standalone pipeline workflows: fallback for pipelines not matched to any binding
- Import ReceiveLocation, IntegrationTrigger, TriggerType
- buildPipelineIntent() now delegates to shared buildPipelineSteps()

Samples:
  ReturnedMail: 6 workflows (no BindingInfo.xml → Pipeline_ fallback — correct)
  BulkUpdateAccounts: 4 workflows (no proper BindingInfo.xml — Pipeline_ fallback)
  UniversoAutoMerge: 2 workflows (no pipelines — unchanged)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix black over-bearing validation — was checking wrong direction

The over-bearing check for black looked at points 23-24 (closer to
bear-off) instead of 19 to from-1 (further from bear-off). This caused
legal over-bearing moves to be rejected during bear-off, making the AI
fall back to random moves or fail mid-sequence.

Also remove debug logging and handle object response format from GNUBG.

commit.message:
Scanner v3: foto pipeline fixes, dual listings, gradient BG, 268 games DB

Phase 1 (Foto Pipeline):
- Back-pairing threshold 0.40→0.28 + partial productcode matching
- Image semaphore leak fix (try/finally pattern)
- compressImage: resize-first approach (2000→1400→1000px steps, then quality loop)
- Lightweight push protection (refuse push if no full-res available)
- Gradient-aware flood fill: follows gradual BG color changes (wooden desks, etc.)
- Quality gate: block auto-push for qualityScore < 25 (blurry photos)

Phase 2 (Scanner):
- Dual condition listings: GB/GBC/GBA/N64 cartridges auto-create "Redelijke staat" variant at 70% price
- Retry-After header parsing from Anthropic 429/503 responses + exponential backoff
- Parallel scanners: mobile 10, desktop 14 (was 6/10)
- Image semaphore: mobile 15, desktop 20 (was 10/15)
- Game database expanded: 180→268 entries (GB/GBC/GBA/DS/3DS/N64/Switch/Wii)
- scan-product route: unified with SYSTEM_PROMPT + dynamic context only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(auth): implementation plan — 7 phase tickets for access redesign [#1020]

Self-contained phase plans with BDD acceptance criteria, file maps,
and context references so any agent can pick up and implement.

Phase 1: Entity restructuring + entitlements (foundation)
Phase 2: Plans + limits + billing foundations
Phase 3: Overrides + advanced limits
Phase 4: Versioning + grandfathering
Phase 5: Billing integration (Stripe)
Phase 6: Cloud storage + data residency
Phase 7: Client-side + UI components

Also moves local-phase-workflow, bdd-acceptance-criteria, and
ticket-system rules from backstage to vertz repo.

fix: use named wildcard for Express 5 SPA fallback

Express 5 / path-to-regexp v8 requires named wildcards. Replace bare

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use condition-based filter degradation for B42 clothing masks

degradeFilterFromInputs tried getDelta/setDelta (DrainableComboItem)
on gas masks, but B42 masks are Clothing items. Accessing non-existent
methods threw in Kahlua, producing stack traces on every safe-variant
hazard recipe craft.

Switched to getCondition/setCondition with probabilistic loss for
low-ConditionMax masks (2-3). ~40 crafts per filter across all mask
types. Added items:size() pre-guard, removed debug print.

commit.message:
Refactor: consolidate boilerplate, auto-discover effects

effects/base.py
- Add _get_params(name): replaces config.get("fx_params",...) in all effects
- Add cx/cy properties: replaces self.W//2 / self.H//2 in draw()
- Add _load_font(sz): single font-loading path, shared by TextStack/MirrorStack

effects/__init__.py
- Auto-discover effects by globbing *.py — no manual _MODULES list needed
- Export POST_MODES as single source of truth (was duplicated in 3 files)

effects/*.py (all 10 effects)
- Remove import config; use self._get_params() from base class
- Use self.cx / self.cy instead of local cx/cy variables
- TextStack/MirrorStack use self._load_font() from base class
- RingTunnel: merge _square_pts/_diamond_pts into single _poly_pts helper
- MorphGeo: replace random.choice([True,False]) with random.random() < 0.5

overlay.py / vjctl.py
- Import POST_MODES from effects instead of redefining _ALL_POST

commit.message:
fix(settings): wire SetupUI subsystem for data provider setup flows

Close 6 wiring gaps preventing provider setups from working end-to-end
(traced via essentials:speedometer → GPS speed provider flow):

1. Setup button visibility uses SetupEvaluator instead of isAvailable —
   shows setup when permissions unsatisfied regardless of GPS hardware
2. Promote evaluateWithPersistence() to SetupEvaluator interface —
   eliminates ClassCastException from `as SetupEvaluatorImpl` in SetupSheet
3. Fix stale snapshot in setup onSettingChanged — read fresh layout state
   per invocation so multi-key writes accumulate correctly
4. Namespace provider setup keys with `setup:{providerId}:` prefix —
   prevents collision with feature settings in the shared settings map
5. Reactive provider availability via connectionState flow collection —
   status dot updates live when GPS/BT toggled while sheet is open
6. effectiveSelection mirrors WidgetDataBinder resolution — prefers first
   available provider instead of unconditional firstOrNull()
7. Fail-fast on unsupported serialization types in SettingsSerialization —
   throws on unknown types, handles Enum (as name string) and Unit,
   keeps j: decode for backward compat

commit.message:
refactor: restructure Application layer to mirror Domain DDD bounded contexts

- Rename Accounts → Identity (mirrors Domain.Identity)
- Merge Roles + Permissions → AccessControl (mirrors Domain.AccessControl)
- Rename Tokens → TokenManagement (mirrors Domain.TokenManagement)
- Move AssignRoleToUser command to Identity context (mutates Account aggregate)
- Move AuditAI concrete implementations to Infrastructure/AI/V3/
- Add missing V3 implementations: AgenticRAGService, MultiHopRetriever, ConfidenceScorer
- Add missing V3 implementations: AuditAgentV3, AgentState, StateGraph, all nodes, AgentTracer
- Register full V3 agentic pipeline in AiExtensions DI
- Add PermissionCreatedEventHandler and PermissionDeletedEventHandler
- Move RoleSettings to AccessControl/Settings, AppClaimTypes to Common/Security
- Fix phantom Common.DTOs namespace → SharedKernel.Common.DTOs
- Remove dead code: empty interfaces, commented-out mapping profile, duplicate RoleCreatedEvent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Google STT audio focus conflict + increase transcription timeout

Google STT was immediately cancelling because:
1. We request AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT
2. SpeechRecognizer internally also requests audio focus
3. Our listener gets AUDIOFOCUS_LOSS → auto-stops recording
4. Result is empty → "No speech detected" → voice mode exits

Fix: Skip requestAudioFocus() for Google STT mode — SpeechRecognizer
manages its own audio focus.

Also increased local transcription timeout from 60s to 180s — benchmark
shows 96s for 3s audio on some devices (32x real-time factor).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tab reorder drag by restoring no-drag on tabs container

The inherited -webkit-app-region: drag on the tabs container was
intercepting HTML5 drag events, preventing tab reorder drops.
Restored no-drag on the container and added a programmatic window
drag handler for empty space clicks between/beyond tabs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: external editor/viewer launch broken on Windows (used /bin/sh)

LaunchExternalProgram was routing through ProcessHelper.RunDetached
which calls /bin/sh -c "..." — doesn't exist on Windows, fails silently.

Rewrite to use ProcessHelper.TryLaunchArgs (direct ProcessStartInfo) which
works on both platforms. Also handles:
- Quoted paths: "C:\Program Files\prog.exe" flags
- Absolute paths with spaces: treated as single executable (no quoting needed)
- Commands with flags: code --wait, gedit --new-window
- Error dialog if the launch fails instead of silent failure

repository.name: logq
repository.description: Fast, interactive log explorer for the terminal

commit.message:
feat: make remote install default to interactive selector (v1.5.1)

One-line installs (curl | bash, bash <(curl ...)) now launch the
interactive menu by reading keyboard input from /dev/tty when stdin
is piped. Centralized terminal detection into can_interact() to
eliminate duplicated checks. confirm() pairs prompt and input on the
same device to prevent prompt/input split.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: awesome-claude-code-config
repository.description: Production-ready Claude Code configuration with self-improvement loop, multi-language rules, MCP integrations, and custom skills

commit.message:
fix(ci): add QREC_EMBED_PROVIDER=stub to MCP test step, fail-fast: false

MCP server pre-loads embedder at startup — without stub it would try to
download the local model (HF 401). Also set fail-fast: false so both
matrix jobs run independently for clearer failure diagnosis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
